steps.title: Combine only like terms
steps.content: The terms $7x$ and $4x$ are like terms because both have the same variable part. The term $3y$ has a different variable, so it cannot be combined with the $x$-terms.

steps.title: Simplify the expression
steps.content: Add only the coefficients of the $x$-terms and keep the $y$-term separate: $$ 7x+4x+3y=11x+3y $$ This matches the expression formed by combining like terms without changing the variable parts. The answer is C.
